I'm new to health insurance I'm a current HBF member I'm thinking of switching to HBF I'm visiting from overseas Learn more about health insurance Find trusted advice and support to help you choose the right cover for you. How to choose the right extras cover Read more 3 things to ask yourself before you buy hospital cover Private health insurance FAQs and how it works Visit our blog for more Why trust HBF for your health insurance? We're not-for-profit We’re one of the largest not-for-profit health funds in Australia and with no shareholders to pay, we focus on giving more back to our members. We’ve been in the business of health since 1941 We’ve got over 80 years’ experience supporting our members’ health and wellbeing. Mix and match Mix and match our hospital and extras products to create cover that’s right for you. A health insurer that puts members first? We’ve got proof, not just promises Benefits of HBF membership HBF health members get access to great discounts and health programs. HBF Member Perks HBF members get access to a range of offers as part of our Member Perks program. You’ll find discounts on gym memberships, shopping, entertainment and more*. Browse offers Health programs Eligible HBF members* can get help staying healthy or managing a health condition. Learn more Pharmacy services Eligible HBF members can use their extras cover for flu vaccinations, health checks and more. Frequently asked questions What is health insurance? Also known as health cover, health insurance is an umbrella term for the types of insurances available to help cover your healthcare costs in Australia: hospital insurance and extras insurance. Hospital insurance Also known as hospital cover, hospital insurance helps cover some of the costs when you're admitted to hospital. It can help cover some of the costs for things like doctors' fees, accommodation and theatre fees. Extras insurance Also known as ancillary cover or extras cover, extras insurance helps cover some or all of the costs for everyday healthcare services including things like dental check-ups, physio appointments and glasses or contacts. These services are generally not covered by Medicare. With HBF, you can choose to purchase hospital or extras insurance on their own, or you can buy both to create a combined policy. Learn more: What is extras insurance? What is hospital insurance? Also known as hospital cover, hospital insurance helps cover costs when you're admitted to hospital. It covers things like your doctors' fees, accommodation and theatre fees. Learn more about hospital cover Also known as ancillary cover or extras cover, extras insurance helps cover the cost of everyday healthcare services including things like dental check-ups, physio appointments and glasses or contacts. These services are generally not covered by Medicare. Learn more about extras cover Who does health insurance cover? You can get health cover for yourself, you and your partner, or for your whole family. Generally, there are four categories of membership: 1 adult (single) 2 adults (couple) 2 adults and any number of dependant*children (family) 1 adult and any number of dependant*children (single parent) How long can my kids stay on my policy? Your kids can stay on your policy if they meet the criteria of a dependant. A dependant is a child who is under the age of 21 and is: covered by a family or single parent policy; and is not married or living in a de-facto relationship. At HBF we also choose to cover dependants under 25 years of age provided they meet the criteria in the 2 points noted above and they: are a full-time student; or earn no more than $24,500 taxable income per calendar year. Can I purchase maternity cover with no waiting period? No. HBF, like most health funds, have a 12-month waiting period for pregnancy and birth. Waiting periods help to discourage people from signing up for health cover, claiming and leaving soon after, which drives premiums up for all other members of the health fund. What is a not-for-profit health fund? HBF is a not-for-profit health fund. A not-for-profit uses any money it makes to further its business objectives and cover the cost of running the business, like paying utilities bills and staff. Any surplus earnings (profits) are also used to help further the not-for-profit's mission. For-profit businesses have the same overheads as not-for-profits, however they use some or all profits to pay shareholders or investors. What is 'community rating'? In Australia, insurance is either 'community rated' or 'risk rated'. Health insurance is 'community rated'. Community rating is part of private health insurance legislation and dictates that everyone living in the same state is entitled to get private health insurance and pay the same base price for the same health insurance policies. This means health funds cannot set a person's health insurance premium using individual factors like age, gender, lifestyle or physical condition. Still have questions? However you want to connect with us, we’re here for you. Call 133 423 Request a callback Email us HBF acknowledges the Traditional Owners of the lands where we live and work. We want to play our part in ensuring that our shared presence brings genuine benefit to First Nations people. View our Reconciliation Action Plan to learn more. About Provider Support Legal Contact Download the HBF App © 2026 Copyright HBF Health Limited - (ABN) 11 126 884 786 and its subsidiaries. Accessibility Sitemap HBF Private health members can mix and match our hospital and extras products as well as get access to discounts and health programs.

His parent (off to the side, softly blurred) watches with relieved posture. Implied before/after via contrast: a neatly aligned tray of sterile tools fades into a radiant, confident smile. Professional trust through crisp surfaces, soft blues and whites, and calm expressions. 50mm prime, f/2.8, shallow depth of field; candid moment, nobody looking at camera. Correct anatomy, natural hands and limbs, natural skin tones, sharp focus, clean composition, uncluttered background. Safe, realistic lighting. No extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements.</Prompt3>
<Prompt4>Australian physio clinic: a man in his 40s performs a guided shoulder stretch while a physiotherapist supports his arm with careful alignment. Relief softens his face as tension eases; a neat rack of bands and a treatment model blur into the background. Implied before/after through lighting: cooler tones near the unused equipment; warmer, focused light on the supported joint. Professional, reliable care in an everyday setting, no luxury staging. 35mm prime, f/2.8, shallow depth of field; candid interaction, subjects not looking at camera. Correct anatomy, natural hands and limbs, natural skin, sharp focus, clean composition, uncluttered background. Safe, realistic lighting. No extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements.</Prompt4>
<Prompt5>Dusk on a suburban Australian street: paramedics carefully load a patient onto a stretcher beside an unbranded ambulance (no text). A partner stands nearby, holding a phone at a natural angle; the screen reflects streetlights (no visible UI). Emphasis on reassurance: steady hands, calm coordination, neat equipment. Before/after implied by cool blue twilight in the background and warm, focused light on the patient’s secure position. Professional, reliable support without drama. 35mm prime, f/2.8, shallow depth of field; candid, no one looks at camera. Correct anatomy, natural hands and limbs, natural skin, sharp focus, clean composition, uncluttered background. Safe, realistic lighting. No extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements.</Prompt5>

Given a landing page URL and its extracted content fields, your task is to generate five (5) high-quality English image generation prompts for Native Ads.

First, reason about the product inside <think>...</think> tags. Extract the following from the landing page:
- ProductType: Physical Product / Digital Product / Service
- SpecificProduct: concise noun phrase
- Category: broad product/service category
- VisualAnchors: 2-3 specific physical elements implied by the page
- LifestyleVibe: emotional tone of the experience
- CoreValueSignals: up to 3 from [professional, premium, affordable, efficient, reliable, simple]

Then output exactly 5 prompts. Each prompt must:
- Be <=150 words
- Embed all safety, realism, quality, and exclusion constraints
- Feel native and non-promotional
- Show the product outcome or value naturally in context
- Avoid stereotypes, text/logos in image, and stock-photo aesthetics
- Ensure correct anatomy, natural hands, sharp focus, clean composition

Output format:

<Prompt2>Digital poster style, no hardware, no humans: floating, layered interface fragments in a luminous, minimalist space. At center, a frosted-glass search bar pair symbolizing origin/destination (location pin + airplane icon), above a semi-transparent calendar grid with two softly glowing dates. Around them, three destination photo tiles (beach, skyline, mountains) rendered as matte acrylic panels, gently tilting in 3D space with Z-axis depth. Thin light ribbons converge toward the center, implying a single, efficient search. High-end rendering: light and glass materiality, soft bloom, crisp edges, no legible text, no logos, no watermark, no promotional slogans. Symmetric, editorial composition, clean, balanced color. 50mm lens aesthetic, f/2.8 shallow DOF, sharp focal center, background bokeh. <Prompt3>Arrival outcome scene in a bright, budget-friendly hotel lobby: a traveler (late 20s, curly hair, neutral attire) rolls a compact suitcase toward the elevators, not looking at camera, relaxed posture. Foreground focal point on the check-in counter: a car key fob and room keycard beside a phone at a natural angle displaying a tidy, generic itinerary UI with icons for flight, hotel, and car, plus a subtle date range—elements minimal and not legible. Soft afternoon light, gentle reflections on polished surfaces, clean composition, uncluttered background signage (no readable text). 50mm prime lens, f/2.8, shallow depth of field aligning with hand and phone. Correct anatomy, natural hands, no logos, no brand names, no watermark. Communicate affordable, efficient, simple through compact luggage, streamlined desk, and calm mood.</Prompt3>
<Prompt4>Window-seat serenity that implies smart savings: a traveler’s relaxed hand rests on a tray table beside a phone angled naturally, screen showing a minimal, generic price trend line descending next to a small calendar icon—no text, no brands, not legible. Outside the window: wing and sunset clouds with warm glow. Subject does not look at camera; scene focuses on comfort after planning done right. Clean composition, selective focus on hand and phone, smooth bokeh in cabin and sky. 35mm prime lens, f/2.8 shallow DOF, natural reflections on screen, correct anatomy, natural hands, no airline logos, no watermark, no extra text, uncluttered background. Convey affordable, efficient, simple through calm posture, tidy tray, and balanced light.</Prompt4>
<Prompt5>Top-down flat lay of a simple kitchen table during planning: two friends (diverse skin tones, casual sweaters) lean in with relaxed posture, faces partially visible and not looking at camera. Nearby: neatly packed passport wallet (no readable text), reusable mug, compact carry-on by chair leg, soft morning light. Clean composition, uncluttered background, subtle reflections on screen, depth-of-field consistent with hand proximity. 35mm prime lens, f/2.8, sharp focus where intended. Generate 5 image generation prompts for a Native Ad based on the following landing page information: [Landing Page URL] https://www.udemy.com/how-to-write-a-research-paper/ [Document Title] How to Write an Effective Research Paper | Udemy [Visual Title] How to Write an Effective Research Paper [Heading] How to Write an Effective Research Paper What you'll learn Course content Instructor Introduction 1 lecture • 1min How to Efficiently Conduct Your Research 8 lectures • 33min Understanding How a Research Paper is Written 4 lectures • 28min How to Write Each Section of Your Paper 18 lectures • 1hr 18min Course Summary 1 lecture • 7min Requirements Description Who this course is for: Students also bought Frequently Bought Together Learn how to write award-winning research papers with easy steps. Includes examples and a research paper template.Rating: 4.8 out of 52594 reviews2.5 total hours32 lecturesAll LevelsCurrent price: $18.99Original price: $94.99 Write a Killer Literature Review Writing a literature review doesn't have to be the hardest part of your thesis or dissertation.Rating: 4.1 out of 5474 reviews1 total hour11 lecturesAll LevelsCurrent price: $12.99Original price: $34.99 How to Write a Successful Research Paper Complete Research Paper Writing Blueprint With Instructions And Templates For Every Section. Master Academic WritingRating: 4.5 out of 53527 reviews2 total hours45 lecturesAll LevelsCurrent price: $14.99Original price: $69.99 Instructor Mohammad Noori 4.8 course rating 3K ratings [Title (CB)] how to write an effective research paper udemy [Best Snippet (CB)] Over the last decade, the expectation for publishing research papers has increased significantly in academia, in professional communities, and in science and technology discipline [Meta Description] Learn How to Write Research Paper Effectively: Writing Research Papers. Over 32 lectures and 2+ hours of video content. Preview the course free now. [Page Content] Business Communication Research Paper Writing Preview this course Learn how to write award-winning research papers with easy steps. Includes examples and a research paper template. Bestseller 4.8 (2,594 ratings) 13,264 students Created by Mohammad Noori Last updated 4/2013 English English [Auto], Simplified Chinese [Auto] 80% off 3 days left at this price! Add to cart 30-Day Money-Back Guarantee Full Lifetime Access Gift this course Apply Coupon MT260126G2A is applied Udemy coupon Apply Learn how to properly and efficiently conduct a research project, especially for scientific research Learn exactly how to prepare an effective research paper for publication in a scientific journal, archival volume, or other professional publications By following the methods and suggested approaches, you will be able to write an effective and possibly award-winning paper that communicates your findings with your professional community in a clear and organized manner Research Paper Writing Communication Business 5 sections • 32 lectures • 2h 26m total length Expand all sections Instructor Introduction Preview Chapter 1 Overview Defining the Research Question / Theme / Topic Gathering Background Information - Locate Prior Works Gathering Background Information - Read Key Papers Gathering Background Information - Organize References Forming a Hypothesis and an Outline Summarizing the Analysis / Simulation / Experiment Organizing and Interpreting Results Chapter 2 Overview Preliminary Steps Before You Start Paper Structure and Function Title Abstract Key Words Introduction - Overview Introduction - Organization Introduction - First Paragraph Introduction - Middle Paragraphs Introduction - Middle Paragraphs (continued) Introduction - Final Paragraph Introduction - Final Paragraph (continued), and Extra Tips The Methods - Overview The Methods - Examples The Results The Discussion Summary and Conclusion Acknowledgements Appendices References Final Course Review No prior experience in writing a research or technical paper required. This course is equally helpful to beginners as it can help those with some familiarity with research to write more effective research papers. Over the last decade, the expectation for publishing research papers has increased significantly in academia, in professional communities, and in science and technology disciplines worldwide. In scientific fields (especially in academia), both graduate students and faculty are expected to publish the results of their research work in journals and conference proceedings. Publication of research or journal papers has also become a key measure for tenure and promotion assessments. In fact, U.S. News, National Research council, and other external organizations often consider publications as a critical metric in their analysis and ranking of programs and institutions. An increasing number of highly ranked universities, worldwide, now require publication of papers in SCI indexed journals as a per-requisite to receive a doctoral degree. Unfortunately, despite the widespread standard of writing and publishing research papers, most Universities don’t offer any formal training or mentorship on how to write effective papers. Given a landing page URL and its extracted content fields, your task is to generate five (5) high-quality English image generation prompts for Native Ads.

First, reason about the product inside <think>...</think> tags. Extract the following from the landing page:
- ProductType: Physical Product / Digital Product / Service
- SpecificProduct: concise noun phrase
- Category: broad product/service category
- VisualAnchors: 2-3 specific physical elements implied by the page
- LifestyleVibe: emotional tone of the experience
- CoreValueSignals: up to 3 from [professional, premium, affordable, efficient, reliable, simple]

Then output exactly 5 prompts. Each prompt must:
- Be <=150 words
- Embed all safety, realism, quality, and exclusion constraints
- Feel native and non-promotional
- Show the product outcome or value naturally in context

- Ensure correct anatomy, natural hands, sharp focus, clean composition

[Best Snippet (CB)]
The idea of supporting boys and individual learning profiles was hatched by Josephine Bauman Rudd more than 75 years ago. As faculty member, Mrs. Rudd initially sought to help boy

[Meta Description]
The Rudd Center for Learning and Academic Growth is a well-established academic support program that has aided in the academic success of Salisbury.

[Page Content]
Academics Home Rudd Learning Center The confidence and independence my son garnered from his experience in the Rudd Center has transformed his educational experience. His growth into a diligent and accountable student has enabled him to enjoy his classes and will poise him for success in college. - current parent The idea of supporting boys and individual learning profiles was hatched by Josephine Bauman Rudd more than 75 years ago. As faculty member, Mrs. Rudd initially sought to help boys with reading comprehension. Through her work, she became one of the true pioneers in the field of academic support. The Rudd Learning Center is a well-established academic support program that has aided in the academic success of Salisbury School boys since the 1950s. Over the years, the program has grown to become one of the leading learning centers in New England. We guide our boys to become confident, independent learners, who achieve academic success at Salisbury School and beyond. To help students develop strategies to become more independent learners and reach a greater level of academic success. We offer support for a wide range of academic challenges including those related to: Executive function skills Language-based skills (reading, writing, verbal expression) Dyscalculia Dysgraphia Dyslexia Processing speed Working memory Students are enrolled in the Rudd Center based on: Learning profile (including current psycho-educational or neuropsychological evaluations) Academic record Previous teacher recommendations The director of the learning center, the admissions team, and the director of studies review student files to determine Rudd Center enrollment. On average, the Rudd Center services 30% of the student body at any given time over the course of the school year. Salisbury School offers academic accommodations to students who qualify through their psychological-educational or neuropsychological evaluations. Common classroom and testing accommodations offered: Extended time Use of a calculator Use of a laptop Use of audiobooks Foreign language waiver Assistive Technology The Rudd Center for Learning and Academic Growth is a well-established academic support program that has aided in the academic success of Salisbury.

Our mission is to be the BEST dental experience you’ve ever had. Our team is here to serve you and your family with convenient, comprehensive & comfortable dental care. We offer

[Meta Description]
At Jonny Fisher DDS, our mission is to provide the BEST dental experience you've ever had. Offices in Pullman, WA and Lewiston, ID.

[Page Content]
Skip to content PULLMAN, WA. REQUEST AN APPOINTMENT LEWISTON, ID. Home Meet The Team About Us Dental Services Patient Center Finance Options Contact Us LEARN MORE We also provide Pediatric Care, Cosmetic Bonding, Dentures, Community Outreach and much more. Visit our services page to see our full range of comprehensive dental care. Our mission is to be the BEST dental experience you’ve ever had. Our team is here to serve you and your family with convenient, comprehensive & comfortable dental care. We offer the latest technology and dental care at our new facility. We specialize in helping patients with dental anxiety or other barriers to care; we take great pride in providing an amazing experience. We’ve considered every detail to ensure you the most comfortable experience possible, from your child’s first visit to your continued care throughout life. “Haven’t been to the dentist in 5+ years. I was referred to Jonny Fishers by my orthodontist. And they didn’t disappoint. Dr. Harrington was GREAT. She helped come up with a treatment plan to work with Ortho for before and after treatment. Also went in the next day for a cleaning and Monte was amazing! He was very patient and understanding. And even educated me on the differences between different types of cleanings. Didn’t feel judged one bit. Would definitely recommend to anyone who feels scared or has anxiety of the dentist. They make you feel comfortable.” “I honestly can’t say enough good things about Jonny Fisher DDS. I have always had great experiences here – well, as great as possible considering it is for dental work! Monte is an exceptional hygienist. Very personable, thorough, and considerate. Alex is a great assistant and I’ve been very pleased with Dr. Harrington. She is kind, considerate, thorough, and understanding. I highly recommend this dental office.” “Dr. Fisher’s office is the BEST! Everyone there, from the ladies at the front desk area, to the Dental assistants, and the other Dentists working in Dr. Fisher’s office, are excellent. They make me feel comfortable every time. If you’re looking for the best dental experience on the Palouse, Dr. Fishers office is the place to go!” “Monte is the bomb. Dr. Harrington was good with our the kids and both my wife and I. Grace was super patient and helpful with all the kids too. Everyone helped out with our 9- month-old and were happy to have us there. Tiffany was super helpful friendly and understanding with questions about costs and schedules.” “Dr. Ellis and Cameron were amazing at explaining everything every step of the way. Came in with a broken tooth and left with a plan that I fully understand, thanks to their great explanation of what each step entails. Great atmosphere and staff I have found my new regular dentist due to this great experience!” “Great dentistry! Awesome play space for kids. Books and toys made waiting time easy. Shandi was super awesome with our young kids in the dentist chair. And Tiffany scheduled us at the desk and was super efficient and friendly. Everyone was smiling, helpful, and chipper. Great dentistry! Highly recommend.” “I always have a good experience here. The staff are always pleasant and knowledgeable, I feel like I’m in good hands. The office is modern, comfortable, and well put together. A special thanks to Monte for excellent repeated care and candor in our conversations!” “We love, love, love this office! My kids have all loved all the employees and my son (who has needed a lot of work) fell asleep today during fillings and an extraction with Dr. Robison! Thank you so much for being the best dentist and worth the hour’s drive!” Dr. Fisher has spent several years turning a small quaint practice into Pullman’s Premier Dental Practice. His practice has moved twice since he opened to accommodate patients more comfortably and ensure he is providing not only top quality services, but a top quality environment. Dr. Fisher was born in Nebraska, graduated from Germany and now enjoys raising his family in Washington. MEET DR. FISHER Dr. Sykes prides himself on providing patients with the best dental experience he can offer. Patients routinely mention how much they enjoyed the treatment they received under his care. He is truly passionate about dentistry and providing a comfortable, anxiety-free experience for all patients. MEET DR. SYKES Dr. Harrington grew up knowing she wanted to help people and that by doing something in the medical field she could make a difference in people’s lives. Her experience working in a physician’s office and a research lab showed her that dentistry would be a perfect fit, allowing her to combine science and artistry while working with people to improve their lives. MEET DR. HARRINGTON Dr. Ellis was raised locally in Colfax, WA. While he had to move far away for dental school, he and his wife are excited to be back in the area to join our dental family! Dentistry allows Dr. Ellis to do what he loves: work directly with people to improve their lives and live in this area that means so much to his family. Dr. Ellis can’t wait to meet you and give you the best dental experience you’ve ever had! MEET DR. ELLIS Dentistry is a second career for Dr. Robison. After receiving both a bachelor’s and master’s degree in mechanical engineering and more than 10 years in the field, Dr. Robison decided it was time for a career change, and is now part of our growing team of Dental Experts. MEET DR. ROBISON We understand that financial arrangements can be a barrier to the smile you deserve. We offer pre-pay options and accept all major credit cards. Regardless of your dental coverage, we will work with you to create a financial plan that helps you receive the care you need. FINANCIAL SOLUTIONS PAYMENT OPTIONS SUBMIT Pullman Office 650 SE Bishop Boulevard Suite 200 Pullman, WA 99163 info@jonnyfisherdds.com Lewiston Office 937 Bryden Avenue Lewiston, ID 83501 lewiston@jonnyfisherdds.com PULLMAN OFFICE 650 SE Bishop Boulevard Suite 200, Pullman, WA 99163 Phone: 509-332-2366 Saturday & Sunday: Closed LEWISTON OFFICE Phone: 208-746-2668 Tuesday, Saturday & Sunday: Closed © 2025 | Jonny Fisher DDS All Rights Reserved | Accessibility Statement | Privacy Policy | Sitemap Digital Marketing by MOD Marketing At Jonny Fisher DDS, our mission is to provide the BEST dental experience you've ever had. jonny fisher dds dentist in pullman lewiston

Our mission is to be the BEST dental experience you’ve ever had. Our team is here to serve you and your family with convenient, comprehensive & comfortable dental care. We offer

[Meta Description]
At Jonny Fisher DDS, our mission is to provide the BEST dental experience you've ever had. Offices in Pullman, WA and Lewiston, ID.

[Page Content]
Skip to content PULLMAN, WA. REQUEST AN APPOINTMENT LEWISTON, ID. Home Meet The Team About Us Dental Services Patient Center Finance Options Contact Us LEARN MORE We also provide Pediatric Care, Cosmetic Bonding, Dentures, Community Outreach and much more. Visit our services page to see our full range of comprehensive dental care. Our mission is to be the BEST dental experience you’ve ever had. Our team is here to serve you and your family with convenient, comprehensive & comfortable dental care. We offer the latest technology and dental care at our new facility. We specialize in helping patients with dental anxiety or other barriers to care; we take great pride in providing an amazing experience. We’ve considered every detail to ensure you the most comfortable experience possible, from your child’s first visit to your continued care throughout life. “Haven’t been to the dentist in 5+ years. I was referred to Jonny Fishers by my orthodontist. And they didn’t disappoint. Dr. Harrington was GREAT. She helped come up with a treatment plan to work with Ortho for before and after treatment. Also went in the next day for a cleaning and Monte was amazing! He was very patient and understanding. And even educated me on the differences between different types of cleanings. Didn’t feel judged one bit. Would definitely recommend to anyone who feels scared or has anxiety of the dentist. They make you feel comfortable.” “I honestly can’t say enough good things about Jonny Fisher DDS. I have always had great experiences here – well, as great as possible considering it is for dental work! Monte is an exceptional hygienist. Very personable, thorough, and considerate. Alex is a great assistant and I’ve been very pleased with Dr. Harrington. She is kind, considerate, thorough, and understanding. I highly recommend this dental office.” “Dr. Fisher’s office is the BEST! Everyone there, from the ladies at the front desk area, to the Dental assistants, and the other Dentists working in Dr. Fisher’s office, are excellent. They make me feel comfortable every time. If you’re looking for the best dental experience on the Palouse, Dr. Fishers office is the place to go!” “Monte is the bomb. Dr. Harrington was good with our the kids and both my wife and I. Grace was super patient and helpful with all the kids too. Everyone helped out with our 9- month-old and were happy to have us there. Tiffany was super helpful friendly and understanding with questions about costs and schedules.” “Dr. Ellis and Cameron were amazing at explaining everything every step of the way. Came in with a broken tooth and left with a plan that I fully understand, thanks to their great explanation of what each step entails. Great atmosphere and staff I have found my new regular dentist due to this great experience!” “Great dentistry! Awesome play space for kids. Books and toys made waiting time easy. Shandi was super awesome with our young kids in the dentist chair. And Tiffany scheduled us at the desk and was super efficient and friendly. Everyone was smiling, helpful, and chipper. Great dentistry! Highly recommend.” “I always have a good experience here. The staff are always pleasant and knowledgeable, I feel like I’m in good hands. The office is modern, comfortable, and well put together. A special thanks to Monte for excellent repeated care and candor in our conversations!” “We love, love, love this office! My kids have all loved all the employees and my son (who has needed a lot of work) fell asleep today during fillings and an extraction with Dr. Robison! Thank you so much for being the best dentist and worth the hour’s drive!” Dr. Fisher has spent several years turning a small quaint practice into Pullman’s Premier Dental Practice. His practice has moved twice since he opened to accommodate patients more comfortably and ensure he is providing not only top quality services, but a top quality environment. Dr. Fisher was born in Nebraska, graduated from Germany and now enjoys raising his family in Washington. MEET DR. FISHER Dr. Sykes prides himself on providing patients with the best dental experience he can offer. Patients routinely mention how much they enjoyed the treatment they received under his care. He is truly passionate about dentistry and providing a comfortable, anxiety-free experience for all patients. MEET DR. SYKES Dr. Harrington grew up knowing she wanted to help people and that by doing something in the medical field she could make a difference in people’s lives. Her experience working in a physician’s office and a research lab showed her that dentistry would be a perfect fit, allowing her to combine science and artistry while working with people to improve their lives. MEET DR. HARRINGTON Dr. Ellis was raised locally in Colfax, WA. While he had to move far away for dental school, he and his wife are excited to be back in the area to join our dental family! Dentistry allows Dr. Ellis to do what he loves: work directly with people to improve their lives and live in this area that means so much to his family. Dr. Ellis can’t wait to meet you and give you the best dental experience you’ve ever had! MEET DR. ELLIS Dentistry is a second career for Dr. Robison. After receiving both a bachelor’s and master’s degree in mechanical engineering and more than 10 years in the field, Dr. Robison decided it was time for a career change, and is now part of our growing team of Dental Experts. MEET DR. ROBISON We understand that financial arrangements can be a barrier to the smile you deserve. We offer pre-pay options and accept all major credit cards. You are an expert Ad Creative Director and Senior AI Image Prompt Engineer, specialized in high-performing Native Advertisement visuals.

Given a landing page URL and its extracted content fields, your task is to generate five (5) high-quality English image generation prompts for Native Ads.

First, reason about the product inside <think>...</think> tags. Extract the following from the landing page:
- ProductType: Physical Product / Digital Product / Service
- SpecificProduct: concise noun phrase
- Category: broad product/service category
- VisualAnchors: 2-3 specific physical elements implied by the page
- LifestyleVibe: emotional tone of the experience
- CoreValueSignals: up to 3 from [professional, premium, affordable, efficient, reliable, simple]

Then output exactly 5 prompts. Each prompt must:
- Be <=150 words
- Embed all safety, realism, quality, and exclusion constraints
- Feel native and non-promotional
- Show the product outcome or value naturally in context
- Avoid stereotypes, text/logos in image, and stock-photo aesthetics
- Ensure correct anatomy, natural hands, sharp focus, clean composition

Output format:
<think>
ProductType: ...
SpecificProduct: ...
Category: ...
VisualAnchors: ...
LifestyleVibe: ...
CoreValueSignals: ...
</think>
<Prompt1>...</Prompt1>
<Prompt2>...</Prompt2>
<Prompt3>...</Prompt3>
<Prompt4>...</Prompt4>
<Prompt5>...</Prompt5>

<Prompt1>A dynamic side view of a woman carving downhill on a snowy mountain. A vivid orange ski jacket dominates the frame as she passes close by, its taped seams dotted with tiny beads of snow and the adjustable hood snug against the wind. A hint of the powder skirt shows as she leans into the turn. She wears goggles and looks down the slope, posture athletic and relaxed, with gloved hands and arms naturally set for balance. The overcast sky gives soft, even light, and the spray of snow behind her dissolves into a gentle blur. The jacket appears crisp and detailed, with dense fabric texture and matte zippers and snaps that feel sturdy against the cold. Distant riders and trees fade softly in the background, while the bright orange stands clear against the gray-white slope. The moment feels candid and outdoors, an open, uncluttered mountain scene with space around her and a calm, documentary presence.</Prompt1>
<Prompt2>A candid dusk rain scene on a city street. A woman in her mid-thirties steps across a crosswalk, head slightly bowed against the drizzle, not looking toward the viewer. She wears a black quilted hooded coat; rain beads across the water-repellent fabric and gathers along the stitching, while the smooth metal zipper pull catches a warm glint from the streetlights. She lifts both hands to adjust the hood, fingers lightly gripping the edge, posture relaxed and in motion.  Warm halos from streetlights and car tail-lights glow behind her, softly blurred to create gentle depth. The wet pavement holds dim reflections and faint streaks of color, with small ripples where her steps meet shallow puddles. Natural shadows shape the folds of the coat, and the quilting texture is clearly visible. Her makeup is neutral and understated, with a practical everyday look and wind-touched hair beneath the hood. The background stays quiet—buildings and passing cars fall into soft focus—keeping the scene calm and true to an on-location moment in the rain.</Prompt2>
<Prompt3>An extreme close-up of a creamy beige teddy fleece coat collar being tugged higher against a cold morning. A hand gently adjusts the neckline, fingers sinking into the plush fibers, which appear crisp and tactile as they catch the cool daylight. A faint breath mist hangs in the air, hinting at the chill. Behind the subject, a park path and bare winter branches slip into a smooth, quiet blur, keeping attention on the fabric. Colors are natural and subdued, with lifelike skin tones and soft shadows shaping the collar and seam. The face stays out of view, and the moment feels candid and practical—cozy, simple warmth in a calm outdoor setting.</Prompt3>
<Prompt4>A mint ski jacket rests over a simple wooden chair at the center of an après-ski lodge scene, the fabric still damp from the snow. Tiny melted droplets bead and glisten along the sleeve in the warm glow of the interior lights. A small lift pass pocket and the snow gaiter are subtly visible, hinting at its practical design. Through a frosted window, distant mountains appear softly blurred. In the background, a woman, out of focus, removes her gloves with an easy, natural motion, her attention elsewhere. The jacket is in clear focus, showing the weave of the fabric, matte zippers, gentle creases, and a faint sheen where moisture lingers. Warm amber light falls across the wood, with soft shadows adding depth to the quiet room, and the cool mint color stands out against the cozy tones of the lodge.</Prompt4>
<Prompt5>Soft morning light spills into a tidy entryway, casting gentle highlights across a classic black quilted hooded coat hanging on a wooden peg within easy reach. The quilting and smooth fabric show a subtle sheen where the light skims, with neat stitching and a sturdy hood that feel ready for everyday wear. A woman in casual clothes reaches for the coat in side profile, not looking at the camera, keys and a dog leash gathered in her other hand with a natural, relaxed grip. The coat is the visual anchor, crisp and clear while houseplants and the doorframe soften into a quiet blur, with mild shadows giving depth to the scene. Wood grain, the glint of the keys, and the texture of the leash appear true to color, capturing a calm, lived-in moment just before heading out.</Prompt5>

Attention Learn more Escape to the heart of the Catskills, where nature sets the pace and every moment is meant to be savored. Tucked away on the Chapin Estate in The Western Catskills, The Chatwal Lodge, an all-inclusive resort in White Lake, New York, is a former 18,000-acre wildlife reserve steeped in a century of stories. Here, 14 boutique-inspired luxury accommodations invite you to embrace the pines and the beauty of the seasons. A little quiet goes a long way as you relax, recharge, and unwind at your own pace. After a day of exploration, gather for lighthearted games, savor farm-to-table dining, or sip craft cocktails fireside. The Chatwal Lodge now participates in the World of Hyatt loyalty program. Discover exclusive member benefits and rewards designed to make your stay even more memorable. Built for those seeking a quiet and luxurious escape, our lodge offers an atmosphere best suited to adult travelers.  For reservations please call +1 844-820-0258 Choose from 14 extraordinary boutique rooms, each uniquely designed to offer ample space and every luxury you desire. Thoughtfully crafted with hand-carved furnishings and refined rustic details, every room invites you to unwind, explore, and indulge amid the stunning Hudson Valley landscape at our all-inclusive luxury resort. Explore Rooms & Suites As part of The Unbound Collection by Hyatt brand, we invite you to explore storied spaces that create an ideal backdrop for your unforgettable tale. From historic gems to modern marvels, author your next chapter with The Unbound Collection by Hyatt. Explore All Dining (3) The Chatwal Lodge’s main restaurant showcases the best of the Catskills, sourcing from local farms, bakeries, and artisans to create a warm, inviting dining experience and world-class menus that celebrate the region’s bounty, highlighting the freshest seasonal ingredients with creativity and care. Whether you're hosting an intimate meeting, a private dinner, or an open-air cocktail reception, our all-inclusive lodge is perfect for corporate retreats and team-building, offering a seamless blend of luxury, nature, and personalized service. Explore Meeting Spaces in a Virtual Tour Celebrate your special moments surrounded by the whispering woods and breathtaking beauty of the Catskills. Whether you're planning an intimate wedding, a rehearsal dinner, or a private celebration, our venues provide an unforgettable setting. Business Services Concierge Fitness Center Free Internet Access Free Parking Meeting Facilities Valet Parking Resort Property Restaurant On-Site Room Service Pamper your pup at The Chatwal Lodge with a plush, luxe Pendleton dog bed, feeding and drinking dishes and special treats. There is no weight restriction for pets. *We welcome guests with disabilities traveling with their service animals. Service animals are not pets and are not subject to this Pet Policy. For more information, please contact the hotel. We are committed to providing equal access and opportunity for individuals with disabilities. The features also make this hotel more accessible for older individuals with changing abilities to ensure a seamless experience. Our overall goal is to improve usability throughout the hotel for all guests. Parking & Transportation Get a break from everyday life amid the tranquil surroundings of the Catskills. Plan a day of fly-fishing or boating, visit Bethel Woods Center for the Arts, play croquet on the lawn, or take yoga or meditation classes in our serene natural setting. My husband and I were celebrating his graduation from his masters program. We were met with complimentary champagne, chocolate, and fruit. The level of service exceeded my expectations, and the staff frequently anticipated our needs - offering suggestions before we could even ask. The food was truly farm to table. <Prompt4>Dealer office desk, tidy and calm. [Visual Title]
Mold Testing in Michigan

[Heading]
Haven’t noticed any spores? That doesn’t mean they aren’t there. We suggest calling on MichiganPro for a mold inspection if the following circumstances apply to you:

[Title (CB)]
michigan mold testing inspections michiganpro home inspections

[Best Snippet (CB)]
Mold spores are microscopic and can be found virtually anywhere, but they thrive the most and grow quickly in areas where moisture is present. In most instances these spores can b

[Meta Description]
MichiganPro Home Inspections has 100+ years of combined experience performing mold testing and mold inspections in the entire state of Michigan.

[Page Content]
Serving all of Michigan Mold spores are microscopic and can be found virtually anywhere, but they thrive the most and grow quickly in areas where moisture is present. In most instances these spores can be spotted out in the open, such as on the ceiling above your shower or under a sink. Fortunately for the property owner, this usually means a non-invasive mold inspection can be conducted visually to confirm whether the mold is confined to one space or if it has spread. Our experienced home inspectors are experts at mold testing in Michigan. They’ll inspect your entire home for the presence of mold. Water damage has occurred If your home has endured a leaky roof, broken pipes, flooding due to inclement weather, etc., the affected areas took a few days to dry out. That’s plenty of time for the moisture to settle and invite mold growth. If you are purchasing a new home You don’t know if this home has ever experienced water damage and to what extent. Just because you don’t see any red flags doesn’t mean they aren’t hiding. It’s better to be safe than sorry. After mold testing and remediation Even after mold has been confirmed through testing and a remediation plan has been carried out, we would still highly suggest investing in a mold inspection to confirm that the removal company was able to get it all. Studies have revealed that more than 50 percent of American homes have some level of mold in them, so it certainly wouldn’t hurt to have your home or a home that you’re planning to buy tested. Mold inspection and mold testing services from MichiganPro Home Inspections will give you peace of mind and allow you to rest easy knowing that your home is clear, and your family is not exposed to its negative effects. Contact MichiganPro Home Inspections at 800-470-7938 today to arrange to have mold inspection completed on its own or as part of a standard home inspection. Mold testing in Michigan is extremely important to your health and requires expert attention. With nearly 100 years of collective experience behind us, our experts are sure to get to the bottom of your mold problem. MichiganPro Home Inspections has 100+ years of combined experience performing mold testing and mold inspections in the entire state of Michigan.

Given a landing page URL and its extracted content fields, your task is to generate five (5) high-quality English image generation prompts for Native Ads.

- ProductType: Physical Product / Digital Product / Service
- SpecificProduct: concise noun phrase
- Category: broad product/service category
- VisualAnchors: 2-3 specific physical elements implied by the page
- LifestyleVibe: emotional tone of the experience
- CoreValueSignals: up to 3 from [professional, premium, affordable, efficient, reliable, simple]

Then output exactly 5 prompts. Each prompt must:
- Be <=150 words
- Embed all safety, realism, quality, and exclusion constraints
- Feel native and non-promotional
- Show the product outcome or value naturally in context
- Avoid stereotypes, text/logos in image, and stock-photo aesthetics
- Ensure correct anatomy, natural hands, sharp focus, clean composition

Once you’ve finalised your booking, you’ll receive a confirmation email. It depends on the ferry operator you book with as to how your boarding pass is issued and collected. Whilst some ferry operators issue the boarding pass as soon as your booking is confirmed, others may ask you to check in at the port. There’s also the option for online check-in, which can be easily done via the Direct Ferries app, if available. The ticket desks are usually located near the ferry terminals. To avoid any delays at the port, we’d recommend allowing some extra time to collect your tickets and to check-in. During check-in, you must show your email confirmation, as well as an official photo ID, such as a passport or identity card. You might also be asked to provide documentation for your pet (vaccines, pet insurance etc.) or proof of residence, if you have booked your ticket with a resident discount. The best time to book ferries from Redang Island (Teluk Kalong Jetty) to Lang Tengah Island (Summerbay Resort Jetty) is usually as early as possible. By booking in advance, you’ll not only get your tickets at the best available prices, but you’ll also get a better choice of departure times and cabin/seating options. Tip: Always check the operator’s timetable during holiday periods and factor in queueing time at busy ports. <Prompt1>A dynamic side view of a woman carving downhill on a snowy mountain. A vivid orange ski jacket dominates the frame as she passes close by, its taped seams dotted with tiny beads of snow and the adjustable hood snug against the wind. A hint of the powder skirt shows as she leans into the turn. She wears goggles and looks down the slope, posture athletic and relaxed, with gloved hands and arms naturally set for balance. The overcast sky gives soft, even light, and the spray of snow behind her dissolves into a gentle blur. The jacket appears crisp and detailed, with dense fabric texture and matte zippers and snaps that feel sturdy against the cold. Distant riders and trees fade softly in the background, while the bright orange stands clear against the gray-white slope. The moment feels candid and outdoors, an open, uncluttered mountain scene with space around her and a calm, documentary presence.</Prompt1>
<Prompt2>A candid dusk rain scene on a city street. A woman in her mid-thirties steps across a crosswalk, head slightly bowed against the drizzle, not looking toward the viewer. She wears a black quilted hooded coat; rain beads across the water-repellent fabric and gathers along the stitching, while the smooth metal zipper pull catches a warm glint from the streetlights. She lifts both hands to adjust the hood, fingers lightly gripping the edge, posture relaxed and in motion.  Warm halos from streetlights and car tail-lights glow behind her, softly blurred to create gentle depth. The wet pavement holds dim reflections and faint streaks of color, with small ripples where her steps meet shallow puddles. Natural shadows shape the folds of the coat, and the quilting texture is clearly visible. Her makeup is neutral and understated, with a practical everyday look and wind-touched hair beneath the hood. The background stays quiet—buildings and passing cars fall into soft focus—keeping the scene calm and true to an on-location moment in the rain.</Prompt2>
<Prompt3>An extreme close-up of a creamy beige teddy fleece coat collar being tugged higher against a cold morning. A hand gently adjusts the neckline, fingers sinking into the plush fibers, which appear crisp and tactile as they catch the cool daylight. A faint breath mist hangs in the air, hinting at the chill. Behind the subject, a park path and bare winter branches slip into a smooth, quiet blur, keeping attention on the fabric. Colors are natural and subdued, with lifelike skin tones and soft shadows shaping the collar and seam. The face stays out of view, and the moment feels candid and practical—cozy, simple warmth in a calm outdoor setting.</Prompt3>
<Prompt4>A mint ski jacket rests over a simple wooden chair at the center of an après-ski lodge scene, the fabric still damp from the snow. Tiny melted droplets bead and glisten along the sleeve in the warm glow of the interior lights. A small lift pass pocket and the snow gaiter are subtly visible, hinting at its practical design. Through a frosted window, distant mountains appear softly blurred. In the background, a woman, out of focus, removes her gloves with an easy, natural motion, her attention elsewhere. The jacket is in clear focus, showing the weave of the fabric, matte zippers, gentle creases, and a faint sheen where moisture lingers. Warm amber light falls across the wood, with soft shadows adding depth to the quiet room, and the cool mint color stands out against the cozy tones of the lodge.</Prompt4>
<Prompt5>Soft morning light spills into a tidy entryway, casting gentle highlights across a classic black quilted hooded coat hanging on a wooden peg within easy reach. The quilting and smooth fabric show a subtle sheen where the light skims, with neat stitching and a sturdy hood that feel ready for everyday wear. A woman in casual clothes reaches for the coat in side profile, not looking at the camera, keys and a dog leash gathered in her other hand with a natural, relaxed grip. The coat is the visual anchor, crisp and clear while houseplants and the doorframe soften into a quiet blur, with mild shadows giving depth to the scene. Wood grain, the glint of the keys, and the texture of the leash appear true to color, capturing a calm, lived-in moment just before heading out.</Prompt5>

[Title (CB)]
find our best ev tariffs for your electric vehicle

[Best Snippet (CB)]
What is an EV tariff and how do they work? EV tariffs are designed for electric vehicle owners who charge their cars at home. Unlike other energy tariffs, EV tariffs work with sma

[Meta Description]
Enjoy cheap electric vehicle charging with British Gas and take advantage of off-peak pricing between 12am-5am. Charge smarter, pay less.

[Page Content]
Got an electric vehicle? Our EV tariff lets you enjoy cheaper EV charging at night at just 9p per kWh between 12am-5am. If you're already with us, it's easy to switch to our EV tariff in your online account. Not with British Gas yet? Get started with a quote. Excellent Based on 320,131 reviews Fixed tariff came to end good choice of fixed tariffs to change to easy pro... paul sansom, June 13 Very easy to transfer over, reason for move to go on ev tariff. Just waitin... Stephen Ross, May 23 I have never had a bad experience with British Gas and have been with them... Mike Trott, April 10 I have just changed our car to an EV . Was informed about the electric vehi... Steven Mellor, January 18 I'm on the EV rate . I'm finding it very economical and I'm very happy wit... Trevor Gridley, January 17 I’ve tried them all—Eon, EDF, Octopus, Npower, Scottish Power—but you just... Iain Corcoran, January 14 Verified Agent was able to resolve configuration questions related to Ev tariff and... D Elderfield, December 10 Changed our tariff to electrical vehicle, went through the British Gas app... shopperlady1960, Good company refunded the excess payments I had made, also great overnight... Paul Rattray, November 12 I recently moved to an EV tariff having bought an electric vehicle. The mov... Mr keith lathwell, October 25 Recently decided on a EV and was able to change myself online to the tariff... Lesley Keene, October 23 My tariff was coming to an end British gas showed me the options with proje... D OWEN, October 8 Redirected Can't fault them to be honest. Been with them a while and using the EV driv... Adrian Craske, July 29 Very good service, upgraded my old nonworking smart meters for free so I ca... Steve, July 22 Very good rates for EV drivers , very helpful advisors and so far have made... Michael Price, June 26 Showing our favorite reviews EV tariffs are designed for electric vehicle owners who charge their cars at home. Unlike other energy tariffs, EV tariffs work with smart meters to offer lower electricity rates during off-peak hours – like the British Gas EV tariff, where you get super-cheap electricity between 12am and 5am every day. The higher daytime rates are balanced out by lower nighttime costs, helping you save money when you charge your EV overnight. If you have both an EV charger and a home battery, Charge Power is the tariff for you. It offers half price electricity from 12am to 5am1 every night and is designed for homes with solar and battery systems. You can charge your EV and your home battery overnight, helping you to save money and lower your carbon footprint. If you’re a new customer, join us on any electricity tariff. Then once we’ve set up your account and connected your smart meter, you can switch to an EV tariff in your online account. If you’re an existing customer, simply login to your online account and change to our EV tariff. An electric vehicle you charge at home. We may ask you to prove this A smart electricity meter. If you don’t have one yet, we’ll take care of that for you for free To pay by Direct Debit We’ll arrange a switch date with your current provider, with no disruption to your energy supply We’ll let you know when your switch to us is complete and when to send us your first meter reading. When you get a quote for our EV tariff, we’ll estimate your annual costs. We base this on how much electricity you’ve used in the past. If we don’t know this, we’ll base it on the average amount EV owners normally use. EV owners typically charge their vehicles about three times a week during off-peak hours, and twice a fortnight during peak hours using 10 kWh of electricity each time. Our estimate assumes that 33% of the total electricity you use is during off-peak hours. Remember that factors like your car’s age, charging habits and how else you use electricity at home could mean your actual yearly costs differ from our estimate. Charging at home is often cheaper than using public charging stations, and the savings get even bigger if you’re on an EV tariff. It works with any make of EV and home charger From 12am-5am every day, you’ll get a super low off-peak price for electricity. That's 35 off-peak hours every week to charge your car for less We keep the daytime rate on our EV tariff as close as possible to our normal electricity rates, so you get great value all day We offer one, easy-to-understand EV tariff designed for all EV drivers 9p per kWh between 12am – 5am There are no rules on what you can use the cut-price electricity for during off-peak hours. So, as well as charging your EV during off-peak hours, you could: Use higher-energy appliances, like washing machines and dishwashers Charge up your smartphones, laptops and tablets Use your charger or EV app to schedule your charges during the cheaper nighttime hours Get a free smart electricity meter upgrade to help keep an eye on your spending Want to know how much you could save with an EV tariff? Our savings calculator does just that. It'll show you the annual and per-charge savings you could make compared to your current electricity tariff, and using public chargers. We'll even show you the estimated reduction in CO2 emissions versus a traditional petrol vehicle. To estimate what you could save, just enter your car model, expected annual mileage, and how much you're currently paying for your electricity (if you’re not sure, we can compare costs to current Ofgem price cap rates). You'll be able to see just how much you can save every time you charge, and what that adds up to over a year. Step 1 of 3 To calculate your estimated EV Tariff savings, first select the make and model of your EV* from the drop-down options below. Select your vehicle make: Peugeot Select your vehicle model: e-5008 *EV vehicle models are up to date as of January 2026. Newer models may not be included in the database. We're making EV charging cheaper for customers who purchase a Hive EV charger, wherever they are. Not only can you enjoy cost-effective charging at home, but we've also partnered with Zapmap, the UK's leading EV public charging service, to take savings beyond your driveway. New customers get £20 in credit, the equivalent to 100 free miles, to use at thousands of public charging points nationwide. We won Best Overall Improvement in the Uswitch Energy Awards 2024 & 2025. That's because we performed best – out of all energy suppliers – at improving our overall customer satisfaction scores this year. You will receive a 50% discount on the tariff unit rate for electricity you usebetween 12am and 5am each day. You will still pay the full daily standing chargeapplicable to your tariff. Tariff prices are subject to change.​ Enjoy cheap electric vehicle charging with British Gas and take advantage of off-peak pricing between 12am-5am. Charge smarter, pay less.

<Prompt3>Cozy evening living room, warm lamplight. In the foreground, a window frames the focal point: EV outside connected to a home charger, cable neatly arranged, gentle indicator glow. Natural reflections on the windowpane, accurate skin tones, correct anatomy, natural hands and limbs. Clean, uncluttered setting, no visible branding. No extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements, avoid readable screens. Realistic lighting and materials.</Prompt3>
<Prompt4>Tidy garage at dawn. Focal point: a slim home battery cabinet and inverter with clean cabling, EV plugged in beside it. Dynamic side view, 50mm prime lens, f/2.8 shallow depth of field; sharp focus on battery enclosure and charging cable, soft bokeh on driveway and sky. Subtle surface reflections, accurate textures, no brand marks visible. Natural perspective, clean composition, uncluttered background. No people, no extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements, avoid readable screens. Realistic materials, true-to-life color, no over-processing.</Prompt4>
<Prompt5>Nighttime driveway scene: focal point is an EV plugged into a wall-mounted home charger, LED ring softly glowing, tidy cable looped. Cool blue off-peak moonlight washes the car and charger; through warm-lit windows, a calm, sleeping home. Rooftop solar panels read as a subtle silhouette against the sky. The outcome feels effortless: quiet, affordable overnight charging already in progress. Dynamic side view, 50mm prime lens, f/2.8 shallow depth of field, sharp focus on the port and plug, soft bokeh on house and street. Natural reflections on car paint and charger casing; no visible branding. Realistic materials, true-to-life color, clean composition, uncluttered background. No people, no extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements, avoid readable screens. Correct proportions, no distortions, no floating objects.</Prompt5>

[Title (CB)]
air conditioning company augusta ga

[Best Snippet (CB)]
All Weather Heating and Cooling has been a trusted name in HVAC services for over 25 years, providing unparalleled expertise in the Greater Augusta, GA area. Our commitment to exc

[Meta Description]
If you're looking for reliable HVAC solutions from a renowned air conditioning company in Augusta, GA or a nearby area, turn to All Weather Heating and Cooling!

[Page Content]
Home Heating & Cooling Indoor Air Quality Humidity Control Financing About Contact Get Directions HVAC Service* All Weather Heating and Cooling has been a trusted name in HVAC services for over 25 years, providing unparalleled expertise in the Greater Augusta, GA area. Our commitment to excellence is reflected in our A+ BBB rating, a testament to our dedication to quality service and customer satisfaction. As a locally and family-owned business, we take pride in offering personalized solutions that cater to your unique needs. Our full-service approach ensures that we can handle any heating and cooling challenge, from installations and repairs to maintenance and emergency services. We are available 24/7 to address urgent issues, providing peace of mind when you need it most. - CoreValueSignals: up to 3 from [professional, premium, affordable, efficient, reliable, simple]

- Be <=150 words
- Embed all safety, realism, quality, and exclusion constraints

- Ensure correct anatomy, natural hands, sharp focus, clean composition

The idea of supporting boys and individual learning profiles was hatched by Josephine Bauman Rudd more than 75 years ago. As faculty member, Mrs. Rudd initially sought to help boy

[Meta Description]
The Rudd Center for Learning and Academic Growth is a well-established academic support program that has aided in the academic success of Salisbury.

[Page Content]
Academics Home Rudd Learning Center The confidence and independence my son garnered from his experience in the Rudd Center has transformed his educational experience. His growth into a diligent and accountable student has enabled him to enjoy his classes and will poise him for success in college. - current parent The idea of supporting boys and individual learning profiles was hatched by Josephine Bauman Rudd more than 75 years ago. As faculty member, Mrs. Rudd initially sought to help boys with reading comprehension. Through her work, she became one of the true pioneers in the field of academic support. The Rudd Learning Center is a well-established academic support program that has aided in the academic success of Salisbury School boys since the 1950s. Over the years, the program has grown to become one of the leading learning centers in New England. We guide our boys to become confident, independent learners, who achieve academic success at Salisbury School and beyond. To help students develop strategies to become more independent learners and reach a greater level of academic success. We offer support for a wide range of academic challenges including those related to: Executive function skills Language-based skills (reading, writing, verbal expression) Dyscalculia Dysgraphia Dyslexia Processing speed Working memory Students are enrolled in the Rudd Center based on: Learning profile (including current psycho-educational or neuropsychological evaluations) Academic record Previous teacher recommendations The director of the learning center, the admissions team, and the director of studies review student files to determine Rudd Center enrollment. On average, the Rudd Center services 30% of the student body at any given time over the course of the school year. Salisbury School offers academic accommodations to students who qualify through their psychological-educational or neuropsychological evaluations. Common classroom and testing accommodations offered: Extended time Use of a calculator Use of a laptop Use of audiobooks Foreign language waiver Assistive Technology The Rudd Center for Learning and Academic Growth is a well-established academic support program that has aided in the academic success of Salisbury.

THE ANCIENT IMPERIAL CAPITAL Hue, a city steeped in history, culture, and imperial grandeur, is a mesmerizing gem in the heart of Vietnam. This captivating destination, located on the banks of the Perfume River, boasts an illustrious past as the former imperial capital of the Nguyen Dynasty. Its historic charm is elegantly showcased through its ancient citadel, ornate temples, and regal tombs, providing a window into the rich tapestry of Vietnamese heritage. Although the 130-year- old garden house has changed hands several times, the ownership of An Hien surprisingly would always be the royalty, aristocracies and nobilities in the Imperial City. <Prompt1>Photorealistic macro of a roofer’s gloved hands setting a new slate tile snugly against gleaming copper flashing on a residential roof. Fresh slate edges align in crisp rows; worn, older tiles recede softly in the background, implying the before/after contrast. Morning natural light skims the textures, highlighting precision and craftsmanship. Unbranded roofing hammer and slate cutter in frame, slight motion blur on placement for kinetic realism. 50mm prime, f/2.8, shallow depth of field, natural perspective, clean composition, uncluttered background. Candid: no faces toward camera, correct anatomy, natural hands and limbs, realistic materials and weathering. No studio backdrop, no floating objects. No extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements.</Prompt1>
<Prompt2>Peace-of-mind outcome scene: a small residential home at golden hour after rain, rooflines perfectly straight with new slate and crisp ridge caps; copper downspout channels droplets cleanly. Warm interior window glow hints at comfort; trimmed landscaping and clean driveway enhance premium feel. Before/after implied by a discreet stack of bagged old materials near the curb in soft blur. 50mm prime, f/2.8, shallow depth of field, sharp on roof edges and gutter, gentle bokeh elsewhere. Realistic weather, reflections, and shadows; clean composition, natural perspective. No people facing camera. No studio backdrop, no floating objects. No extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements.</Prompt2>
<Prompt3>Close-up lifestyle shot of newly installed copper gutter and flashing after a light rain, water beading and running cleanly into the downspout on a tidy suburban eave. Warm wood soffit and fresh shingles form orderly lines; a pile of old materials sits softly blurred on the ground, hinting at the upgrade. Late-afternoon natural light makes the copper glow with premium warmth. 50mm prime, f/2.8, shallow depth of field, sharp subject, gentle bokeh of trees. Clean composition, realistic reflections, no distortion. No people looking at camera; if hands appear, correct anatomy, natural pose. No studio backdrop, no floating objects. No extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements.</Prompt3>
<Prompt4>On-site roof repair action: two roofers in clean, unbranded safety gear align asphalt shingles along chalk lines, a nailer mid-fire with subtle motion blur; neat rows show restored order while a small tarp with removed shingles sits blurred behind. Overcast bright sky for even light, emphasizing precision. 35mm prime, f/2.8, shallow depth of field; focus on the crisp shingle edge and tool contact point. Candid: no one looks at camera; correct anatomy, natural hands and limbs. Realistic materials, safety harness visible, uncluttered composition, natural perspective. No studio backdrop, no floating objects. No extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements.</Prompt4>
<Prompt5>Dynamic side view of a freshly restored cedar shake roof catching golden-hour light—rich, oiled wood grain transitioning to older gray shakes farther up the slope, subtly implying before/after. On the ground, a homeowner (30s–50s, casual clothing, natural body type) looks up appreciatively, not at camera, holding a mug; expression calm, protected. Soft clouds receding after rain. 35mm prime, f/2.8, shallow depth of field: cedar texture in crisp focus, homeowner gently blurred. Correct anatomy, natural hands, candid posture. Uncluttered yard, tidy worksite details (coiled hose, bagged debris) in soft bokeh. No studio backdrop, no floating objects. No extra text, no logos, no watermark, no UI elements.</Prompt5>

It might not always be standard home insurance that you need. The policy that's right for you will depend on whether you own, rent, or are a landlord for the property you’re insuring. Owning your own property means you’re responsible for everything. Plus if you have a mortgage, it might be a condition that you have at least buildings insurance. Most renters only need contents insurance as building cover is the landlord's responsibility. You might also want your own renters insurance policy if you share your home with other tenants. Landlords are responsible for providing happy homes to their tenants. But to cover your properties, you might need specialist landlord insurance. If you let your property on a short-term basis, either privately or through platforms like Airbnb, you need short-term let insurance rather than standard home cover. As a student living away from home, you might want student insurance cover for any expensive kit you own. But, check whether your belongings are covered by your parents’ home insurance first. Protecting your second home is straightforward with holiday home insurance, as it’s tailored to the unique risks of letting your property to guests. Note, a second home can’t be covered by traditional home insurance. Home insurance comes in two parts: buildings insurance and contents insurance. These can be bought individually or together as a single policy. Buildings insurance This covers the structure of the property and its permanent fixtures and fittings, like fitted kitchen units and bathroom suites. It might also provide cover for driveways, patios, walls and fences, and any outbuildings. Contents insurance This covers your household goods, valuables, and personal belongings. This includes things like your home appliances, carpets, furniture and jewellery. The items must belong to yourself, your fellow policyholders, or any other residents insured by the policy. Combined insurance A combined buildings and contents insurance policy covers the structure of your home and your belongings. It can be cheaper than taking our separate buildings and contents insurance policies. Join over one million customers getting a home insurance quote with Go.Compare each year. Read our guide on what home insurance covers for more details. Fire damage - Damage caused by home fires, smoke or explosion. General wear and tear - Damage caused by the general passing of time e.g. worn carpets or loose roof tiles. Theft - The replacement of your possessions if lost during a burglary. Accidental damage - For instance, accidentally drilling through a pipe or spilling a glass of red wine on a rug. Weather damage - Extreme-weather damage such as storms, flooding, hail, and lightning strikes. Mechanical breakdown - Appliances such as fridges or washing machines expiring due to age. Escape of water - Water leaking from burst pipes or appliances. Negligence - If not taking action to repair something leads to more damage. Like not fixing a cracked pipe or damp patch. Malicious damage - Intentional damage to your home, like vandalism, as long as you or anyone staying with you did not cause it. Poor workmanship - Damage that’s caused due to a low standard of building work or DIY, for example sub-par wiring led to a fire. Subsidence, heave or landslip - Damage to your home caused by subsidence, heave or landslip. Empty properties - Unoccupied property cover may be needed as an add-on, as it poses a greater risk of damage or theft. Falling trees - Damage to your home, like broken fences or damaged roof tiles, caused by a tree falling. Loss away from home - Loss of items on your person while you're away from your home Covers the cost of emergency repairs if your boiler breaks down, your pipes burst or your roof falls off. It can also help with broken windows, busted locks and even pest infestations. You are an expert Ad Creative Director and Senior AI Image Prompt Engineer, specialized in high-performing Native Advertisement visuals.

Given a landing page URL and its extracted content fields, your task is to generate five (5) high-quality English image generation prompts for Native Ads.

First, reason about the product inside <think>...</think> tags. Extract the following from the landing page:
- ProductType: Physical Product / Digital Product / Service
- SpecificProduct: concise noun phrase
- Category: broad product/service category
- VisualAnchors: 2-3 specific physical elements implied by the page
- LifestyleVibe: emotional tone of the experience
- CoreValueSignals: up to 3 from [professional, premium, affordable, efficient, reliable, simple]

Then output exactly 5 prompts. Each prompt must:
- Be <=150 words
- Embed all safety, realism, quality, and exclusion constraints
- Feel native and non-promotional
- Show the product outcome or value naturally in context
- Avoid stereotypes, text/logos in image, and stock-photo aesthetics
- Ensure correct anatomy, natural hands, sharp focus, clean composition

Output format:

RightSure for Business Landlord Insurance Instant quotes, easy online purchase, and an unbeatable price match promise* Price Match Promise* Safe & Secure UK Based Specialists Quote & Buy Online 24/7 High Level of Standard Cover At RightSure For Business, we understand that managing rental properties comes with its own challenges. From finding reliable tenants to protecting your investment during void periods, there’s a lot to consider, and having the right insurance in place is essential. Our specialist Landlord Insurance protects your property and income against risks like fire, flood, theft, accidental damage, and loss of rent. Whether you own a single property or multiple rentals, we’ll help you find cover for your property and budget. If you find a cheaper quote for an equivalent policy within two days of purchase and can provide written proof, you may cancel with us and receive a full refund, provided no claims have been made. That’s our promise: competitive cover, guaranteed peace of mind. Landlord insurance offers financial protection to property owners renting to tenants. There are three main types of insurance available: Buildings Insurance – this covers your bricks and mortar. It covers damage to the structure of your property as well as the rebuild cost if it is damaged beyond repair. Landlord Contents Insurance – The cost of repairing or replacing the furniture, fixtures, and fittings you provide in your rental property is covered by landlord’s contents insurance. The level of cover will depend on if you’re letting your property as furnished Liability Insurance – provides protection to landlords if you, your tenant or guests become injured in your property. There are a number of other add-ons which maybe useful to have. Want to discuss further? Call our award winning Landlord Insurance Specialists on 0800 515 381. Whether you manage a single buy-to-let or a portfolio of rental properties, we can help you find the right insurance for your needs. You are an expert Ad Creative Director and Senior AI Image Prompt Engineer, specialized in high-performing Native Advertisement visuals.

Given a landing page URL and its extracted content fields, your task is to generate five (5) high-quality English image generation prompts for Native Ads.

First, reason about the product inside <think>...</think> tags. Extract the following from the landing page:
- ProductType: Physical Product / Digital Product / Service
- SpecificProduct: concise noun phrase
- Category: broad product/service category
- VisualAnchors: 2-3 specific physical elements implied by the page
- LifestyleVibe: emotional tone of the experience
- CoreValueSignals: up to 3 from [professional, premium, affordable, efficient, reliable, simple]

Then output exactly 5 prompts. Each prompt must:
- Be <=150 words
- Embed all safety, realism, quality, and exclusion constraints
- Feel native and non-promotional
- Show the product outcome or value naturally in context
- Avoid stereotypes, text/logos in image, and stock-photo aesthetics
- Ensure correct anatomy, natural hands, sharp focus, clean composition

Output format:
